jokes.text: Andy asking about Michael's 'favorite men's magazines'
jokes.setup: Andy trying to get information about his new boss
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and inappropriate question about men's magazines
jokes.explanation: Establishes Andy's character as overenthusiastic and inappropriately personal. The specificity of 'men's magazines' makes it funnier than generic nosiness.

jokes.text: Andy's sudden threat: 'Cross me and I will destroy you'
jokes.setup: Andy being friendly and casual with Jim
jokes.punchline: Immediate shift to melodramatic threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - Andy's fake friendliness masking competitive aggression. The whiplash tone change is expertly executed.

jokes.text: Dwight claiming he outran a black pepper snake and is 'somewhere between a snake and a mongoose. And a panther.'
jokes.setup: Dwight boasting about his running speed
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous animal comparisons, especially the awkward addition of 'And a panther'
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight absurdism. The animal hierarchy is nonsensical but delivered with complete confidence. 'And a panther' as an afterthought is comedic gold.

jokes.text: Jim's talking head revealing the 'stopwatch' is actually a digital thermometer
jokes.setup: Jim appearing to time Dwight's running
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been using a thermometer, not a stopwatch
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim prank psychology - the elaborate setup for something completely pointless. The visual revelation makes the audience complicit in the joke.

jokes.text: Michael's ethnic speculation: 'Probably Italian. Possibly Filipina.'
jokes.setup: Michael meeting Karen Filippelli for the first time
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate ethnic speculation based solely on her last name
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate behavior. The cringe comes from his complete lack of awareness about how inappropriate this is.

jokes.text: Andy's advice to fire Anthony Gardner based on Japanese camp guard philosophy
jokes.setup: Andy giving Michael management advice
jokes.punchline: Comparing office management to WWII Japanese prison camps
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and inappropriate advice delivered with Andy's characteristic overconfidence. The historical reference is both specific and completely wrong for the situation.

jokes.text: Andy's follow-up confessional about being good at choosing who dies
jokes.setup: Andy's prison camp analogy
jokes.punchline: His disturbing confidence that he'd be good at selecting people to die
jokes.explanation: Doubles down on the dark comedy while revealing Andy's sociopathic tendencies. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it more disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: Michael's instruction to set up food 'as if you were trying to impress a much older man who's way out of your league'
jokes.setup: Michael giving directions about food presentation
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and inappropriate romantic metaphor
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to make everything weirdly personal and inappropriate. The specificity of 'much older man' makes it particularly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Michael's 'World's Best Dad' mug justification
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his paternalistic view of his employees
jokes.punchline: Using it to justify having a 'World's Best Dad' mug at work
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional self-perception as a father figure. The mug becomes a physical manifestation of his inappropriate boundary issues.

jokes.text: Gift bags are 'just for guests' - existing employees can 'buy one later'
jokes.setup: Michael giving gift bags to new employees
jokes.punchline: Telling existing employees they can buy their own gift bags
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - creating artificial scarcity and charging employees for 'gifts.' The casual cruelty is delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Tony's gift bag contains 'mostly pencils'
jokes.setup: Tony opening his welcome gift bag
jokes.punchline: The disappointing contents being just pencils
jokes.explanation: The anticlimax of the 'special' gift bag being office supplies. Sets up the low-budget reality of Michael's grand gestures.

jokes.text: Michael asking Karen 'are you a robot or a Martian?' and 'Was your dad a GI?'
jokes.setup: Michael meeting Karen and noting she's 'exotic-looking'
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate questions about her background
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriateness. The escalation from 'exotic' to robot/Martian to military assumptions shows his complete lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: Andy's philosophy: 'Name repetition, personality mirroring, and never breaking off a handshake. I'm always thinking one step ahead, like a carpenter that makes stairs.'
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his strategy to become number two
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical carpenter analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy character work. The carpenter analogy sounds profound but makes no sense, perfectly capturing his pseudo-intellectual confidence.

jokes.text: Andy and Michael's 'nifty gifties' exchange
jokes.setup: Michael giving Andy a gift bag
jokes.punchline: Their mutual appreciation of the made-up phrase 'nifty gifties'
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's successful personality mirroring strategy in action. Both characters enjoying their fake enthusiasm creates mutual cringe.

jokes.text: Jim's fake introduction: 'I'm new here' followed by immediate acknowledgment of the obvious joke
jokes.setup: Jim pretending not to know his coworkers after returning from Stamford
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaking character to acknowledge the failed joke
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim self-awareness. The meta-commentary on his own failed joke attempt shows his evolved sense of humor and comfort with failure.

jokes.text: Dwight's intimidation attempt with forehead staring
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to assert dominance over Jim's return
jokes.punchline: His bizarre attempt at psychological intimidation through forehead staring
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight weirdness. His attempt at intimidation through eye contact/forehead staring is both absurd and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: The awkward desk situation resolution
jokes.setup: Jim discovering someone (likely Dwight) is sitting at his old desk
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable negotiation and quick resolution
jokes.explanation: Subtle workplace awkwardness. The power dynamics and politeness masking territorial disputes creates realistic workplace comedy.

jokes.text: Andy vs Dwight title battle: 'Assistant Regional Manager' vs 'Regional Director in Charge of Sales'
jokes.setup: Two characters comparing their inflated job titles
jokes.punchline: Andy's film set director comparison and Dwight's movie-watching credentials
jokes.explanation: Great character work showing both Andy and Dwight's competitive nature and inflated self-importance. The film analogy is perfectly pretentious for Andy.

jokes.text: Dwight's movie expertise: 'I've seen over 240 of them'
jokes.setup: Andy challenging Dwight's film knowledge
jokes.punchline: Dwight's oddly specific and unimpressive number
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight response - the specific number sounds impressive to him but is actually quite modest. The confidence with which he delivers it makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Kelly's celebrity baby information dump
jokes.setup: Jim asking Kelly what's new with her
jokes.punchline: Her response being entirely about celebrity babies, then saying that IS what's new with her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly characterization - her complete inability to distinguish between celebrity news and her own life. The earnest delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Michael's food restrictions: 'for you, consider it cow meat... poisoned beef'
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the food is for guests only
jokes.punchline: His increasingly dramatic explanations to prevent employees from eating
jokes.explanation: Michael's petty power play escalating to absurdity. The progression from 'cow meat' to 'poisoned beef' shows his desperation to maintain control.

jokes.text: Michael's 'bore-ientation' pun dismissing Toby's materials
jokes.setup: Toby offering orientation materials
jokes.punchline: Michael's dismissive pun about it being boring
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but perfectly captures Michael's need to be the fun boss and his reflexive dismissal of Toby. More about character than comedy quality.

jokes.text: Michael's orientation video - 'Lazy Scranton' rap
jokes.setup: Michael presenting his 'funny' orientation video
jokes.punchline: The terrible rap video with nonsensical lyrics and awkward performance
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael cringe comedy. The rap is simultaneously terrible and memorable, perfectly capturing his delusional confidence in his creative abilities.

jokes.text: Jim's callback to 'The Scranton Witch Project'
jokes.setup: Watching Michael's terrible orientation video
jokes.punchline: Referencing an equally terrible previous video with a perfect parody title
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure and movie parody title. Economically establishes that Michael's terrible videos are a pattern, not an exception.

jokes.text: Michael's self-assessment: 'A-plus-plus' for his video
jokes.setup: Michael evaluating his own terrible rap video
jokes.punchline: His complete delusion about its quality, inventing 'A-plus-plus'
jokes.explanation: Michael's complete disconnect from reality. The invention of 'A-plus-plus' shows his need to exceed even perfect scores for his mediocre work.

jokes.text: Dwight coaching Karen's voicemail message to be 'more Italian'
jokes.setup: Dwight helping Karen with her voicemail
jokes.punchline: His stereotypical Italian accent coaching and inappropriate assumptions
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight inappropriate behavior. The ethnic stereotyping and his confidence in coaching her creates uncomfortable comedy.

jokes.text: Andy's manipulation strategy talking head
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his office politics strategy
jokes.punchline: His calculated plan to marginalize Dwight while appearing charming
jokes.explanation: Reveals Andy's calculating nature behind his friendly facade. The strategic thinking contrasts with his goofy public persona.

jokes.text: Andy's screensaver compliment to Angela
jokes.setup: Andy showing Angela something on his computer
jokes.punchline: It's just his screensaver, but he treats it like a meaningful gesture
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's try-hard nature - making a big deal out of something completely mundane. The earnestness makes it mildly endearing and cringey.

jokes.text: Jim's evolution from grape soda to bottled water
jokes.setup: Pam noticing Jim's changed drink preferences
jokes.punchline: Jim's pretentious explanation about 'evolving' from grape soda
jokes.explanation: Subtle character development joke. Jim's attempt to seem more mature/sophisticated is gently mocked through the trivial nature of the change.

jokes.text: Angela's breast on Andy's computer screen
jokes.setup: People looking at Andy's computer screen
jokes.punchline: Angela's matter-of-fact explanation that it's her breast
jokes.explanation: Shocking visual gag with perfect deadpan delivery from Angela. The casual way she explains it makes it funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Phyllis's pine perfume from 'metropolitan Orlando' and Bob Vance introduction
jokes.setup: Karen complaining about a smell
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's oddly specific perfume origin story and mysterious Bob Vance reference
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific details. 'Metropolitan Orlando' and pine perfume are perfectly Phyllis. Sets up the Bob Vance running gag.

jokes.text: Michael's 'Night at the Roxbury' reference and head bobbing performance
jokes.setup: Michael's integration celebration party
jokes.punchline: His awkward recreation of the SNL skit with technical difficulties
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael dated reference and performance. The technical problems and awkward execution are more cringey than funny, but in character.

jokes.text: Michael's 'nose candy' comment making Karen itchy
jokes.setup: Karen saying her nose is itchy
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate drug reference
jokes.explanation: Weak innuendo humor typical of Michael. The joke is more about his inappropriate nature than the quality of the wordplay.

jokes.text: Tony's inability to get on the table leading to his resignation
jokes.setup: Michael insisting Tony join others on the table for unity
jokes.punchline: The awkward physical struggle and Tony's immediate decision to quit
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Michael's terrible team building. The physical comedy combined with Tony's reasonable response creates great cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Michael firing Tony for quitting: 'That's heresy, my friend!'
jokes.setup: Tony trying to quit on his first day
jokes.punchline: Michael's dramatic overreaction and misuse of 'heresy'
jokes.explanation: Michael's ego can't handle rejection so he turns victim into villain. 'Heresy' is perfectly wrong word choice for the situation.

jokes.text: Angela's Hooters restaurant defense
jokes.setup: Angela criticizing the office environment as like a strip club
jokes.punchline: Andy's earnest defense of Hooters as a legitimate restaurant chain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy response - completely missing the point while being technically correct. His earnest corporate statistics make it funnier.

jokes.text: Michael's fake tire prank to unite the office
jokes.setup: Michael claiming Vance Refrigeration vandalized their cars
jokes.punchline: His elaborate fake prank designed to create false unity
jokes.explanation: Clever Michael manipulation scheme. The fake external threat to create internal unity is psychologically astute and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: The fake hate note: 'You can never pull together as one and revenge us!'
jokes.setup: Michael showing the 'hate note' from Vance Refrigeration
jokes.punchline: The obviously fake note with poor grammar and transparent psychology
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the fake prank setup. The obvious fakeness and psychological manipulation attempt is hilariously transparent.

jokes.text: Bob Vance of Vance Refrigeration introduction pattern
jokes.setup: Karen asking about Bob Vance
jokes.punchline: The repeated full business name introduction becoming a running gag
jokes.explanation: Establishing the Bob Vance running gag. The repetitive full business name becomes a character quirk that will pay off throughout the series.

jokes.text: Michael's lunch lady analogy for bringing people together
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his strategy for office unity
jokes.punchline: His personal story about befriending the lunch lady reveals his childhood loneliness
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly poignant Michael moment. The analogy starts as management theory but reveals his personal loneliness and need for connection.

jokes.text: Karen's corkscrew lobotomy phone call
jokes.setup: Andy asking about Karen's first day
jokes.punchline: Her darkly comic description of wanting to give herself a lobotomy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect specificity. The corkscrew detail makes it both more vivid and more absurd. Great Karen character establishment.

jokes.text: Andy vs Dwight car argument ending in coughing insults
jokes.setup: Andy and Dwight comparing their cars
jokes.punchline: The argument devolving into coughing 'Idiot!' back and forth
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of petty masculine competition. The childish coughing insults show both characters reverting to elementary school behavior.

jokes.text: Andy's 'Cor-Not University' insult
jokes.setup: Andy suggesting Dwight should be an English professor
jokes.punchline: His fake university name 'Cor-Not University'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Andy's pseudo-intellectual tendencies. The made-up university name is juvenile but fits the escalating argument.

jokes.text: Andy's final confessional claiming victory in the argument
jokes.setup: Andy and Dwight's ridiculous car argument
jokes.punchline: Andy's delusional belief that he won the exchange
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy delusion. His confidence that he 'won' an argument that devolved into coughing shows his competitive self-deception.
